title: Window Functions, Analytical Grain, and Time-Aware SQL · Relational Lens: Time Semantics
summary: Service leaders need rankings, running effort, previous-event comparisons, and rolling metrics without collapsing detail rows. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql time semantics increment in a time-aware service-level analytics report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate instants, local civil time, duration, calendar boundaries, and incomplete reporting windows.
Build running and rolling aggregates with explicit ROWS or RANGE frames and edge-case checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Compare each ticket with its team's effort and severity peers, retain teams with no resolved work, and rank ties deliberately. Build named query stages, one changed window of 3 rows, and a reconciliation total. Competency probe: Separate instants, local civil time, duration, calendar boundaries, and incomplete reporting windows. A defensible solution applies Time Semantics, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a time-aware service-level analytics report.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-time-semantics — Separate instants, local civil time, duration, calendar boundaries, and incomplete reporting windows.
SELECT team_id, ticket_id, severity,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY team_id ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id) AS arrival,
       LAG(opened_at) OVER (PARTITION BY team_id ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id) AS previous_opened,
       COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_id) AS team_ticket_count
FROM tickets
ORDER BY team_id, arrival;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Time Semantics evidence · Theory Time Semantics
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-time-semantics-w8yyh3" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; window partition, ordering, frame, rank, or time evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Time Semantics evidence · Theory Time Semantics.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Time Semantics into observable performance evidence. For Build Time Semantics evidence · Theory Time Semantics, keep Time Semantics connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-time-semantics-w8yyh3
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT event_id, ticket_id, minutes_spent,
       SUM(minutes_spent) OVER (PARTITION BY ticket_id ORDER BY occurred_at, event_id ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS running_minutes
FROM ticket_events ORDER BY ticket_id, occurred_at, event_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
